svg.text: 16.2 · The double-entry ledger — the immutable source of truth
append-only · every transaction balanced (Σ debits = Σ credits) · balances DERIVED from entries
immutable ledger_entry (append-only — never UPDATE/DELETE)
transfer $100 from acct A → acct B = ONE atomic txn (M07), TWO balanced entries:
entry: acct A · −10000 minor · txn_42 · 2026-06-28 (DEBIT)
entry: acct B · +10000 minor · txn_42 · 2026-06-28 (CREDIT)
Σ = 0 → money CONSERVED (double-entry invariant)
money = integer minor units (M03 — never FLOAT) · corrections = NEW compensating entries (M01/1.17)
DERIVED balances
balance = Σ the account's
immutable entries
acct A balance_minor (cached)
cached for fast reads, updated
atomically WITH the entries (M07)
…and RE-DERIVABLE → reconciliation
WHY immutable + double-entry + derived (vs a naive mutable balance column)
✓ AUDIT TRAIL (permanent, tamper-evident history — for compliance, 16.8) · ✓ no LOST-UPDATE risk (entries immutable, M15/15.9)
✓ money CONSERVED (Σ=0 per txn) · ✓ balance RE-DERIVABLE → RECONCILIATION can always recompute the truth (16.7, M12/12.14)
the naive mutable balance: no history, lost-update prone, can't prove correctness — WRONG for money
💰 money-never-lies, structural: double-entry → conserved · immutability → provable · derived → re-derivable (reconcilable)
the heart of every fintech platform — the foundation every other challenge builds on (M01/M02/M03)
partitioned by time for retention (M13/13.2) · immutability enforced by privilege (M13/13.14)
